features.description: Fix critical pipeline engine bugs that cause the scheduler to stall after server restart and silently drop user notifications on retry exhaustion. (1) End stale session rows during crash recovery so they don't count against maxParallel. (2) Insert pending_attention rows for all exhausted retry paths (validator_fail, diff_check_fail, post_command_action retry-exhausted) so the user is notified. (3) Add missing exhausted outcomes to TRANSITIONS table for validator_fail and diff_check_fail. (4) Consistently arm retryAfterAt and retryModeFor maps for all paths into awaiting_retry, not just the non-zero exit path. (5) Add test coverage for the handoff-injector module (currently 0 tests). (6) Add scheduler integration tests for crash recovery with session cleanup, restart with items in awaiting_retry/awaiting_user, multi-phase workflow phase advancement, and the post_command_action goto -> handoff injection -> re-spawn loop.
acceptance_criteria: Stale sessions are ended (status='failed') during crash recovery in Scheduler.start(), and stale session rows do not count against maxParallel on restart
All exhausted retry paths (validator_fail, diff_check_fail, post_command_action retry-exhausted) insert a pending_attention row when transitioning to awaiting_user
TRANSITIONS table has explicit exhausted outcomes for validator_fail and diff_check_fail
retryAfterAt and retryModeFor are armed for all paths into awaiting_retry (pre_command_failed, post_command_action, validator_fail, diff_check_fail, crash recovery), not only the non-zero exit path
handoff-injector has unit tests covering: file absent, append to existing, malformed JSON tolerance, output truncation, correct entry shape
Crash recovery test verifies stale sessions are ended AND new sessions can spawn after recovery
Scheduler test verifies restart with items in awaiting_retry correctly fires backoff_elapsed
Scheduler test verifies a two-phase workflow advances current_phase correctly
Scheduler test verifies post_command_action goto injects hook failure into handoff.json and re-spawns
All existing tests continue to pass (no regressions)
review_criteria: endSession is called for every stale session detected by buildCrashRecovery, inside the same recovery loop that fires session_fail
No hardcoded { to: awaiting_user, sideEffects: [] } fallbacks remain in selectConditionalOutcome -- all exhausted paths use explicit outcomes with pending_attention
retryAfterAt arming is centralized (helper function or post-transition check), not copy-pasted in each branch
New tests use real SQLite with migrations, matching the pattern of existing engine.test.ts and scheduler.test.ts
No changes to the state machine's State or Event union types -- this is a fix, not a feature extension
No changes to the API layer or prompt templates -- scope is engine + scheduler + tests only

features.description: Initialize a Vite 6 + React 19 + TypeScript project under src/web/ with Tailwind CSS 4 for styling. Configure vite.config.ts with dev-server proxy forwarding /api and /stream to the backend Fastify server at http://127.0.0.1:{YOKE_PORT}. Set up React Router v7 with route stubs for / (workflow list), /workflow/:workflowId (feature board + stream pane), and /workflow/:workflowId/item/:itemId (item detail). Create an AppShell layout component with a sidebar slot (workflow list), a main content area, and a top bar with connection-status indicator and notification bell. Add pnpm scripts: dev, build, preview, and test:e2e. Include a tsconfig.json with strict mode and path aliases (@/ maps to src/web/).
acceptance_criteria: pnpm --filter web dev starts the Vite dev server and renders the AppShell with sidebar, main area, and top bar
pnpm --filter web build produces a production bundle in dist/web/ with zero TypeScript errors
React Router routes /, /workflow/:workflowId, and /workflow/:workflowId/item/:itemId render distinct placeholder views
Tailwind utility classes apply correctly in dev and production builds
API proxy forwards GET /api/workflows to the backend without CORS errors
WebSocket proxy forwards ws://localhost:{DEV_PORT}/stream to the backend
review_criteria: Vite config uses explicit proxy targets, not wildcard rewriting
TypeScript strict mode is enabled with no any escape hatches in scaffold code
Tailwind is configured with content paths covering src/web/**/*.{ts,tsx}
No unused dependencies in package.json; only approved stack (React, Vite, Tailwind, React Router, @tanstack/react-virtual)
AppShell uses CSS grid or flexbox layout, not absolute positioning
Route definitions are declarative (createBrowserRouter or equivalent), not imperative

features.description: Implement a WebSocket client module at src/web/ws/ that connects to ws://127.0.0.1:{port}/stream, handles the hello frame (validating protocolVersion === 1, disconnecting on mismatch), and exposes subscribe(workflowId, sinceSeq?), unsubscribe(workflowId), sendControl(action, opts), sendAck(sessionId, lastAppliedSeq), and ping() methods. The client generates UUIDv4 commandId values for each ClientFrame.id. Reconnect uses exponential backoff capped at 30s with jitter. On reconnect, the client re-sends subscribe for all active subscriptions with the last-seen seq per session. Incoming ServerFrame objects are typed via a discriminated union on type. The module maintains a per-session high-water-mark map for deduplication: any frame with seq <= hwm[sessionId] is dropped. Expose a connectionState observable (connecting | connected | reconnecting | disconnected | version_mismatch) consumed by the AppShell connection indicator. Cap concurrent streaming subscriptions at 4, surfacing SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT errors to the UI. Handle backfill.truncated frames by extracting the httpFetchUrl and transitioning the subscription into HTTP-paged-backfill mode before switching to live frames. Route error frames by code: PROTOCOL_MISMATCH closes the socket and sets connectionState to version_mismatch, SUBSCRIPTION_LIMIT surfaces to the UI, BAD_FRAME is logged as a client bug, and INTERNAL triggers reconnect. Emit typed events via a callback registry keyed on ServerFrameType.
acceptance_criteria: Client connects, receives hello, and transitions connectionState to connected
Client disconnects with a version_mismatch indicator when hello.protocolVersion !== 1
subscribe(workflowId) sends a correctly-shaped ClientFrame with type subscribe and a UUIDv4 id
On WebSocket close, client enters reconnecting state and retries with exponential backoff (100ms to 200ms to 400ms to 30s cap)
On reconnect, client re-subscribes with sinceSeq equal to the per-session high-water mark
Duplicate frames (same sessionId + seq <= hwm) are silently dropped
sendControl generates an idempotent commandId; sending the same logical command twice within 5 minutes reuses the same id
connectionState is observable by React components via a useConnectionState hook
backfill.truncated frame extracts httpFetchUrl and defers to HTTP paging; the subscription still transitions to live after the paged fetch completes
error frames with code PROTOCOL_MISMATCH transition connectionState to version_mismatch and close the socket
error frames with code INTERNAL trigger a reconnect with backoff, not a permanent disconnect
review_criteria: WebSocket URL is derived from window.location or env config, never hardcoded
Reconnect backoff includes jitter to prevent thundering herd
ClientFrame and ServerFrame types match protocol-websocket.md section 8 type definitions exactly
No raw JSON.parse without try/catch; malformed frames are logged and dropped
The module has no React dependency; it is a pure TypeScript module consumed by hooks
Subscription state is tracked in a Map keyed by workflowId, not component state
Error code routing is exhaustive (switch over known codes with a default → log + reconnect)

features.description: Implement a service worker at src/web/sw.ts registered by the app shell that enables browser push notifications for requires_attention events. The notification permission flow is triggered by an explicit user gesture: clicking the bell icon in the top bar opens a permission prompt exactly once; the result (granted/denied/dismissed) is stored in localStorage and never re-prompted. When permission is granted, notice frames with severity requires_attention dispatch self.registration.showNotification() with the notice kind as the title and message as the body. Clicking a notification fires a notificationclick event that focuses the dashboard tab and navigates to the relevant workflow via deep-link URL. If permission is denied, fall back to an in-app toast notification rendered via a toast stack in the AppShell and show a permanent badge on the bell icon. The service worker handles the beforeinstallprompt event for PWA installability but does not proactively show install prompts.
acceptance_criteria: Service worker registers on app load without errors
Clicking the bell icon for the first time triggers Notification.requestPermission()
Permission result is stored in localStorage; the prompt is never shown again
When granted: requires_attention notices produce a browser notification with kind and message
Clicking a browser notification focuses the dashboard tab and navigates to the workflow
When denied: attention notices produce an in-app toast instead
When denied: the bell icon shows a permanent push-disabled badge
The service worker does not cache app assets (no offline-first; this is a localhost app)
review_criteria: Permission is requested only on explicit gesture, never on page load (anti-pattern: permission nag)
The service worker script is minimal; no workbox, no asset caching, no offline strategy
showNotification is called from the service worker context via postMessage, not from the main thread
Toast fallback renders via a portal-based stack, not alert()
localStorage key for permission state has a namespaced key (e.g., yoke:push-permission)
The notificationclick handler uses clients.openWindow() or clients.matchAll() + focus(), not window.open()

features.description: Implement a normalized render-model reducer at src/web/store/ that consumes typed ServerFrame events from the WebSocket client and produces an ordered array of render blocks per session. The reducer handles five block types: TextBlock (accumulates stream.text deltas by blockId, marks frozen on final: true), ToolCall (created from stream.tool_use, status updated from stream.tool_result keyed by toolUseId, linked input/output), ThinkingBlock (accumulates stream.thinking deltas, collapsed by default), SystemNotice (synthesized from session.started, session.ended, stream.system_notice, notice, stage.started, and stage.complete frames; carries severity and source), and UsageUpdate (from stream.usage, not rendered inline but stored for the HUD). Orphan stream.tool_result without a preceding stream.tool_use triggers a snapshot re-request. session.ended freezes all blocks for that session; no further mutations accepted. The reducer maintains a hard cap of 10,000 events in-memory per session; older blocks are evicted with a truncated sentinel enabling on-demand HTTP fetch from the Session Log Store.
acceptance_criteria: stream.text deltas with the same blockId accumulate into a single TextBlock.text string
A TextBlock with final: true is marked frozen; subsequent deltas for that blockId are dropped
stream.tool_use creates a ToolCall block with status pending; matching stream.tool_result updates status to ok or error and attaches output
Orphan tool_result (no preceding tool_use for that toolUseId) sets a needsSnapshot flag
session.ended freezes the session; subsequent stream frames for that sessionId are rejected
stream.system_notice produces a SystemNotice with correct severity and source mapping
The reducer enforces a 10,000 block cap per session; block 10,001 evicts the oldest block and inserts a truncated sentinel at position 0
stream.usage frames update a per-session usage accumulator accessible via getSessionUsage(sessionId)
stage.started produces a SystemNotice with severity info, source harness, and message showing stage ID, run mode, and item count
stage.complete produces a SystemNotice with severity info showing stage ID, next stage (if any), needsApproval flag, and item summary counts
review_criteria: Reducer is a pure function (state, action) => state with no side effects
Block types use a discriminated union with a type field, not class hierarchies
Delta accumulation uses string concatenation or a rope/buffer, not array join on every render
The 10k cap eviction is O(1) amortized, not O(n) array shift
No React imports in the reducer module; it is framework-agnostic
ToolCall linkage uses a Map keyed on toolUseId, not a linear scan

features.description: Implement a WorkflowList sidebar component at src/web/components/WorkflowList/ that displays workflows using keyset pagination via GET /api/workflows?status=&q=&before=&limit=20. Each row shows workflow name, status chip (color-coded by state-machine label), updatedAt relative timestamp, and unread-event badge count. Real-time updates arrive via workflow.index.update WebSocket frames, which patch the list without requiring a re-fetch. Provide a filter bar with status dropdown (all, active, paused, complete, failed, cancelled), a text search input bound to the q parameter, and an Archived toggle. Clicking a workflow row calls subscribe(workflowId) on the WebSocket client and navigates to /workflow/:workflowId. The active workflow is visually highlighted. Implement infinite scroll: when the user scrolls near the bottom, fetch the next page using the last row's createdAt as the before cursor.
acceptance_criteria: Initial load fetches the first page of workflows and renders rows with name, status chip, relative timestamp, and unread badge
workflow.index.update frames update the matching row in-place (status, updatedAt, unreadEvents) without full list re-fetch
Status filter restricts the list to matching workflows; changing the filter resets pagination
Text search filters by workflow name with debounced (300ms) requests
Archived toggle shows/hides archived workflows
Clicking a row navigates to /workflow/:workflowId and highlights the active row
Scrolling to the bottom loads the next page seamlessly (keyset pagination, not offset)
An empty state message appears when no workflows match the current filters
review_criteria: Keyset pagination uses before cursor, not offset/limit; no skipped or duplicate rows on concurrent inserts
workflow.index.update handler patches the existing list item by workflowId, not by array index
Filter and search parameters are reflected in the URL query string for deep-link support
HTTP requests use AbortController to cancel in-flight fetches when filters change
The component does not poll; it relies on WebSocket frames for real-time updates
Relative timestamps update on a 60s interval, not per-frame

features.description: Implement a CrashRecoveryBanner component at src/web/components/CrashRecoveryBanner/ that renders when WorkflowSnapshotPayload.workflow.recoveryState is non-null. The banner displays: the crash timestamp (recoveredAt), the prior workflow status (priorStatus), the resume method (continue or fresh), whether uncommitted changes exist (uncommittedChanges), and the last known session ID. The banner provides two action buttons: Acknowledge and Resume (sends a control frame with action resume and the resumeMethod from recovery state) and Cancel Workflow (sends action cancel with confirmation dialog). The banner persists until the server clears recoveryState via a workflow.update frame with recoveryState: null. The banner renders at the top of the main content area with a warning-amber background and cannot be dismissed without acknowledgement.
acceptance_criteria: Banner appears when workflow.recoveryState is non-null in the snapshot
Banner displays recoveredAt, priorStatus, resumeMethod, uncommittedChanges status, and lastKnownSessionId
Acknowledge and Resume sends a control frame and shows optimistic spinner state
Cancel Workflow shows confirmation dialog before sending control frame
Banner disappears when workflow.update arrives with recoveryState: null
Banner cannot be dismissed via close button; only clears via server-side state change
Banner renders above all other main-content components using layout order, not modal overlay
When uncommittedChanges is true, the banner shows an additional warning about stashed changes
review_criteria: Recovery state is read from the workflow snapshot, not from a separate API call
The banner does not poll for recovery state changes; it reacts to workflow.update frames
Resume method (continue vs fresh) is passed through to the control frame, not hardcoded
The component uses the same sendControl mechanism as the ControlMatrix, not a separate HTTP path
Warning styling uses semantic Tailwind classes (amber/yellow palette), not inline styles
The banner is rendered in a layout slot that guarantees top-of-content placement

features.description: Implement an AttentionBanner component at src/web/components/AttentionBanner/ driven by the pendingAttention array from WorkflowSnapshotPayload and real-time notice frames with severity requires_attention. Each pending attention item shows its kind, payload summary, and createdAt timestamp. The banner provides an Acknowledge button per item that sends POST /api/workflows/:id/attention/:attentionId/ack to clear it. The banner renders as a stacked list of attention cards below the crash recovery banner (if present) and above the main content. Items arrive via notice frames with persistedAttentionId and are cleared when the server sends a workflow.update removing them from pendingAttention. The bell icon in the AppShell top bar shows a badge count of unacknowledged attention items.
acceptance_criteria: Banner renders pending attention items from the workflow snapshot
New notice frames with severity requires_attention and persistedAttentionId add items to the banner
Acknowledge button sends HTTP POST to /api/workflows/:id/attention/:attentionId/ack and shows optimistic removal with spinner
Acknowledged items disappear when workflow.update confirms removal from pendingAttention
The AppShell bell icon shows a numeric badge matching the count of pending attention items
Badge count updates in real-time as attention items arrive or are acknowledged
Banner is hidden (not collapsed) when no attention items exist
Attention items are ordered by createdAt ascending (oldest first)
review_criteria: Acknowledgement uses the HTTP endpoint (POST .../ack), not a WebSocket control frame
The bell badge count is derived from the attention items array length, not a separate counter
Attention items are never cleared by a push-notification failure; only by explicit acknowledgement
The component does not poll; it reacts to WebSocket frames for additions and workflow.update for removals
Optimistic removal is display-only; if the HTTP POST fails, the item reappears
The banner stacks below CrashRecoveryBanner using layout order, not z-index manipulation

features.description: Implement a GithubButton component at src/web/components/GithubButton/ that renders the GitHub PR state from WorkflowSnapshotPayload.workflow.githubState. The button reflects the full GithubState.status enum: disabled (hidden), unconfigured (greyed out with setup hint tooltip), idle (ready indicator), creating (spinner), created (link to PR with state badge: open=green, merged=purple, closed=red), and failed (error message display). When status is created, display prNumber as a clickable link to prUrl with a state chip. The lastCheckedAt timestamp shows as a relative-time tooltip. State updates arrive via workflow.update frames patching githubState. The button is rendered in the workflow header area alongside the workflow name and status chip. All six status values are handled exhaustively.
acceptance_criteria: Button is hidden when githubState.status === disabled
Button shows greyed-out state with setup hint when status is unconfigured
Button shows spinner when status is creating
Button shows PR link with number and state badge when status is created
PR state badge colors: open=green, merged=purple, closed=red
Button shows error text when status is failed
workflow.update frames updating githubState change the button state in real-time
lastCheckedAt renders as a relative timestamp tooltip on hover
review_criteria: All six GithubState.status values are handled exhaustively (switch with no default fallthrough)
PR URL is rendered as an <a> tag with target=_blank and rel=noopener noreferrer
The component does not make HTTP requests; all state comes from WebSocket frames
Status transitions are idempotent; receiving the same status twice does not cause flicker
Error display is bounded (text truncated with title tooltip), not unbounded height
The component is read-only; GitHub retry requires server-side action outside this component's scope

features.description: Implement a LiveStreamPane component at src/web/components/LiveStream/ that renders the normalized render-model blocks for the active session using @tanstack/react-virtual with variable-size rows and measure-on-mount via measureElement. Each block type has a dedicated renderer: TextBlockRenderer (monospace pre-wrap with syntax highlighting for fenced code blocks), ToolCallRenderer (collapsible with name header, input JSON tree, and result), ThinkingBlockRenderer (collapsed by default, muted indigo accent, expandable), SystemNoticeRenderer (left-rule accent colored by severity: info=blue, warn=amber, error=red). Follow-tail behavior: auto-scroll when the viewport is within 50px of the bottom; detach on upscroll (>50px from bottom); show a Jump to latest pill when detached. Text deltas are batched and flushed via requestAnimationFrame at 16ms intervals to prevent per-character re-renders. The truncated sentinel block renders as a Load earlier messages button that fetches from GET /api/sessions/:id/log. Persist scroll position in a ref keyed by sessionId so tab switches restore position.
acceptance_criteria: The pane renders 1,000+ blocks without layout jank (no dropped frames at 60fps in Chrome DevTools)
Variable-height blocks (short text vs long code block) measure correctly and do not overlap
Follow-tail auto-scrolls as new blocks arrive when the user is at the bottom
Scrolling up detaches follow-tail; a Jump to latest pill appears
Clicking Jump to latest scrolls to the bottom and re-engages follow-tail
ThinkingBlock renders collapsed by default; clicking expands inline without disrupting scroll position
ToolCall shows name, input preview, and status badge; expanding shows full input JSON and result
Load earlier messages button fetches older blocks from the HTTP endpoint and prepends them without scroll jump
Switching between sessions preserves and restores scroll position
review_criteria: Uses @tanstack/react-virtual useVirtualizer with estimateSize and measureElement, not fixed-height rows
Text delta batching uses a single requestAnimationFrame loop, not per-block timers
Block renderers are memoized (React.memo) with stable keys derived from blockId
No dangerouslySetInnerHTML for text content; code highlighting uses a safe library or pre-tokenized approach
The component does not subscribe to the full render-model state; it receives blocks for the active session only
Scroll position restore does not trigger a re-render cascade

features.description: Implement a FeatureBoard component at src/web/components/FeatureBoard/ that renders items from the workflow.snapshot payload grouped by category (from items_display config). Each item card shows displayTitle, displaySubtitle, status chip (from ItemStateProjection.status), currentPhase label, and retryCount badge if > 0. Items with blockedReason show a warning icon with tooltip. Real-time updates arrive via item.state frames, patching the card in-place. Provide a fuzzy search bar (client-side filter over displayTitle + displaySubtitle), a status filter dropdown, and a category filter. Support deep-link URLs: /workflow/:workflowId/item/:itemId scrolls to and highlights the target item. Implement keyboard navigation: j/k move focus between items, Enter opens the item's stream pane, Escape clears selection. Pin the currently-streaming item (the item whose session is active) to the top of its category group with a pulsing indicator. Selecting an item for detail view triggers an item.data request (the opaque user data blob is not included in workflow.snapshot per protocol §2.2); the returned ItemDataPayload is displayed in a collapsible detail section within the item card or a side panel, rendered as a JSON tree.
acceptance_criteria: Items render grouped under category headers from the workflow snapshot
Each card displays displayTitle, status chip, currentPhase, retryCount badge, and blockedReason warning
item.state frames update the matching card without full board re-render
Fuzzy search filters items client-side by displayTitle and displaySubtitle
Status and category dropdowns filter the displayed items
Navigating to /workflow/:workflowId/item/:itemId scrolls to and highlights the specified item
j/k keyboard navigation moves a visible focus ring between items; Enter opens the stream pane for the focused item
The currently-streaming item is pinned to the top of its group with a visual indicator
Selecting an item triggers an item.data request; the opaque data blob renders in a collapsible JSON tree
item.data is fetched once per item selection and cached client-side; re-selecting the same item does not re-fetch
review_criteria: Category grouping uses a Map<string, Item[]> derived from snapshot, not nested array traversal on each render
item.state handler uses immutable update (spread + replace) keyed on itemId, not array index
Keyboard navigation uses aria-activedescendant pattern for accessibility, not manual focus management
Deep-link scroll uses scrollIntoView({ block: center }) after the board has rendered, with a layout-effect guard
Fuzzy search is debounced (200ms) and runs client-side against the in-memory item list, not the server
The component unsubscribes from item.state events on unmount
item.data cache is invalidated when a new item.state frame arrives for the same itemId (data may have changed)

features.description: Implement a UsageHUD component rendered in the AppShell top bar that displays aggregated token usage for the active workflow. The HUD shows: input tokens, output tokens, cache read tokens, and cache creation tokens as compact numeric displays with abbreviated formatting (e.g., 12.3k, 1.2M). Per-session usage is accumulated from stream.usage frames via the render-reducer's getSessionUsage(sessionId) accessor. Workflow-level totals are computed by summing across all sessions in the current workflow (from snapshot activeSessions + completed sessions). The HUD updates in real-time as stream.usage frames arrive. Clicking the HUD expands a dropdown showing per-session usage breakdown with session phase labels.
acceptance_criteria: HUD displays input, output, cache read, and cache creation token counts for the active workflow
Token counts use abbreviated formatting (k/M suffixes) with one decimal place
Counts update in real-time as stream.usage frames arrive
Workflow total sums across all sessions (active + completed from snapshot)
Clicking the HUD toggles a dropdown with per-session breakdown
Per-session rows show session phase label and individual token counts
HUD shows a dash when no usage data is available
The dropdown closes on outside click or Escape key
review_criteria: Usage accumulation is in the render-reducer, not in the component
Abbreviated formatting uses a utility function, not inline arithmetic
The dropdown is positioned with a portal to avoid overflow clipping
Token counts are stored as numbers, formatted only at render time
The HUD does not re-render on every stream frame; only on stream.usage frames
Per-session breakdown is derived from the reducer state, not a separate data fetch

features.description: Define the deep-link URL convention and implement route-level handling for incoming deep links from macOS native notifications (dispatched server-side via node-notifier). Deep-link URLs follow the pattern http://127.0.0.1:{port}/workflow/:workflowId?attention=:attentionId for attention items and http://127.0.0.1:{port}/workflow/:workflowId/item/:itemId for item-specific links. When the dashboard loads with these URL patterns, it auto-subscribes to the referenced workflow (if not already subscribed), scrolls to the relevant item or attention banner, and applies a brief highlight animation (2s pulse) to draw the user's eye. If the workflow is not found (deleted or invalid ID), render a Workflow not found error state with a link back to the workflow list. This feature is client-side routing only; the server-side notification dispatch is handled by the backend agent.
acceptance_criteria: Loading /workflow/:workflowId?attention=:attentionId subscribes to the workflow and scrolls to the attention banner
Loading /workflow/:workflowId/item/:itemId subscribes and scrolls to the referenced item card
A 2-second pulse highlight animation draws attention to the deep-linked element
If the workflow ID is invalid, a Workflow not found error page renders with a back link
Deep links work on initial page load (cold start) and on in-app navigation
The attention query parameter is consumed and cleared from the URL after processing
Deep-link routing does not duplicate the subscribe call if the workflow is already subscribed
review_criteria: Deep-link handling is implemented at the route level (loader or effect in the route component), not in a global listener
The highlight animation uses CSS animation/transition, not JavaScript timers manipulating DOM styles
URL cleanup uses history.replaceState to remove consumed query params, not a redirect
Subscription deduplication checks the WebSocket client's active subscription set
Error state for invalid workflow uses the same layout as other error states, not a blank page
No server-side code is introduced; this feature is purely client-side routing

features.description: Implement a ReviewPanel component at src/web/components/ReviewPanel/ that specializes the stream pane rendering for review-phase sessions. When the active session's phase is review, the panel detects Task tool_use calls (identified by ToolCall.name === 'Task') and renders each as a collapsible subagent row. Each row shows the subagent's task description (extracted from ToolCall.input), a status strip (pending, running, ok, error), and nested stream output from the subagent. Subagent runs are rendered sequentially in invocation order. The panel provides a summary header showing total subagents, pass/fail counts, and overall review status. Non-Task tool calls within a review session render using the standard ToolCallRenderer. The panel is activated by the phase field on SessionStartedPayload; non-review sessions render in the standard LiveStreamPane.
acceptance_criteria: Review-phase sessions render in ReviewPanel instead of the standard stream pane
Task tool_use calls render as collapsible subagent rows with task description header
Each subagent row shows status (pending/running/ok/error) with color-coded indicator
Subagent rows display in invocation order
The summary header shows counts: total subagents, passed, failed, pending
Expanding a subagent row shows its nested stream output (text, tool calls, thinking blocks)
Non-Task tool calls in a review session render with the standard ToolCallRenderer
Collapsing/expanding subagent rows does not disrupt scroll position
review_criteria: Phase detection uses SessionStartedPayload.phase, not heuristics on stream content
Task tool_use identification is exact (name === Task), not substring match
Subagent pass/fail is derived from ToolCall.status (ok/error), not from parsing output text
The component reuses ToolCallRenderer and TextBlockRenderer for nested content, not custom renderers
Summary counts update reactively as subagent results arrive
The panel does not hold subagent state outside the render-model reducer

features.description: Extend the LiveStreamPane and render-reducer to handle prepost.command.* frames within the session stream. prepost.command.started inserts a SystemNotice with source hook showing the command name, phase, and when (pre/post). prepost.command.output appends stdout/stderr chunks to a collapsible log region under the started notice, styled with a left-rule accent and monospace font, differentiated by stream field (stdout=default, stderr=red). prepost.command.ended finalizes the notice with exit code and resolved action summary. The render-reducer maps these frames to SystemNotice blocks with attached output buffers keyed by runId. An orphan prepost.command.ended without a preceding started triggers a snapshot re-request (same invariant as orphan tool_result).
acceptance_criteria: prepost.command.started renders as a SystemNotice with command name, phase, and pre/post label
prepost.command.output chunks append to a collapsible log region beneath the started notice
stdout and stderr are visually differentiated (stderr in red text)
prepost.command.ended finalizes the notice with exit code (green for 0, red for non-zero) and action label
Output chunks are buffered and flushed at 16ms intervals (same rAF batching as text deltas)
Collapsing the output region does not disrupt scroll position or follow-tail
Orphan ended frame (no preceding started for that runId) triggers needsSnapshot
Pre-command and post-command output interleaves correctly with other stream content in chronological order
review_criteria: Pre/post frames are mapped to SystemNotice blocks in the render-reducer, not handled in the component
Output buffering reuses the same rAF flush mechanism as TextBlock deltas
runId keying uses a Map in the reducer, not a linear scan
Stderr rendering uses a semantic class, not inline color: red
The collapsible region preserves its expanded/collapsed state across re-renders
No new block types are introduced; pre/post rendering uses SystemNotice with extended metadata

features.description: Implement a ControlMatrix component at src/web/components/ControlMatrix/ that renders available manual-control actions based on the current (workflow.status, item.status, session.status) triple. The matrix defines which ControlPayload.action values are valid in each state combination: pause when workflow is in_progress, resume when paused, cancel always except when already cancelled or complete, skip when item is blocked or in_progress, retry when item is failed, unblock when item is blocked, inject-context when session is active, rerun-phase when item phase is complete or failed, and approve-stage when the current stage has needsApproval true and status is complete. Each action button sends a control frame via the WebSocket client with a generated commandId. Buttons display optimistic state (disabled + spinner) until the server acknowledges via a corresponding workflow.update or item.state frame. Destructive actions (cancel, skip) require a confirmation dialog. The matrix is derived from a declarative rules object, not hardcoded if/else chains.
acceptance_criteria: Available actions update dynamically as workflow, item, and session status change
pause button appears only when workflow is in_progress; clicking sends a control frame with action pause
cancel button shows a confirmation dialog before sending the control frame
After sending a control, the button shows a spinner and disables until server state reflects the change
commandId is generated once per action invocation and reused on retry (idempotency)
inject-context opens a text input modal; the entered text is sent as extra in the control payload
approve-stage appears only when StageProjection.needsApproval is true and stage status is complete
Invalid actions for the current state triple are hidden, not merely disabled
review_criteria: Control availability is derived from a declarative rules object mapping action to (workflow, item?, session?) => boolean, not imperative conditionals
Optimistic state is display-only; the component does not mutate any state store
Confirmation dialog uses a controlled component pattern, not window.confirm
commandId generation uses crypto.randomUUID(), not Math.random()
The component does not directly import the WebSocket module; it receives a sendControl callback via props or context
Destructive action buttons are visually distinct (red/warning treatment)
